findings.title: Timeline View is Fixed and Not Scalable
findings.summary: The piano roll timeline is a vast improvement but is currently fixed to a 4-bar width. It does not account for phrases or sections that are longer than four bars, which would cause syllables to be positioned incorrectly or overflow without a scrolling mechanism.
findings.recommendation: The design must incorporate horizontal scrolling for the timeline. Both the beat ruler and the syllable track area should scroll in unison. The underlying implementation will need to generate the timeline grid width and syllable positions dynamically based on the total duration of the musical section, not a fixed value.

findings.title: Syllable Text Editing Workflow is Ambiguous
findings.summary: Editing the main phrase text is now possible, but the consequence for the underlying syllables is not defined. The system needs a way for users to manually correct automated syllabification (e.g., splitting 'ec-stat-ic' differently).
findings.recommendation: Introduce a dedicated syllable editing mode. For example, a double-click on a syllable could transform its text label into an input field, allowing the user to refine the text or use a special character (like '|') to indicate a split or merge point.

findings.title: Properties Panel Creates Redundant Workflows
findings.summary: The new properties panel is helpful but duplicates editing capabilities that would be more intuitive if performed directly on the syllable. Editing dynamics and beat timing requires moving the cursor away from the object of interest.
findings.recommendation: Prioritize direct manipulation. Beat changes should be primarily draggable on the timeline. Make the dynamics badge on the syllable itself interactive (e.g., clickable to cycle values, or right-click for a context menu). The panel should be for secondary, high-precision edits.

findings.title: Vocal Direction Input is Prone to Error
findings.summary: Using a free-form text input for 'direction' tags invites inconsistent data entry ('whisper', 'Whisper'). These are likely to be recurring, standardized terms.
findings.recommendation: Replace the free-form text input with a more structured component, such as a tag input field with an autocomplete dropdown of existing values, or a simple dropdown if the list is short and fixed. This ensures data consistency.
